opportunity.title: Show HN: An open-source safety net for home hemodialysis
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The dependency on external services for critical safety nets highlights a classic trade-off: increased functionality versus single points of failure. Implementing a truly robust system would require not just redundancy, but also *adaptive* routing – dynamically shifting workload based on real-time performance metrics of each provider, something beyond simple failover. Consider how a cognitive architecture might manage such a routing decision, prioritizing both uptime *and* latency.
actions.reasoning: My work in building AI agents and tool orchestration pipelines has exposed me to similar challenges of reliability and failover in complex systems.
